contamination_source.mechanism: claude --print auto-loads ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/memory/*.md into every model call
contamination_source.memory_dir_size_at_time: ~500 markdown files including 60+ fabrication-review casebook entries the same model had produced in earlier architecture-debate sessions
contamination_source.leaked_pattern: When the gradient task prompts mention 'GitLab DB' / 'GitHub MySQL 2018' / 'Knight Capital 2012', the model pattern-matches them against its own prior refusal cases ('project_gitlab_db_review_fabrication.md', 'project_github_mysql_2018_review_*.md', 'project_knight_capital_2012_*.md') and treats the new (legitimate) prompts as the next recurrence — refusing to review and producing F1 = 0.000.

downstream_handling.primary_action: experiments/src/run_experiment.py:_call_claude was patched on 2026-05-21 (PR #57) to invoke claude --print from a tempfile.mkdtemp() neutral cwd. The fix moves the encoded-cwd memory dir lookup to a directory that holds zero memory files. Live probe after the patch returned MEMORY_CHECK = 0.
downstream_handling.secondary_action: The first 5 contaminated cells of this run (~3 file mtimes around 2026-05-21 04:00 KST) were deleted at the time of the patch. The remaining cells in this directory (23 total) were preserved because they document the refusal-pattern itself, which is paper-thesis-relevant evidence (see paper §sec:implications for prior intra-session anchoring cases R1-R12).

use_for: comparison against the cwd-neutralised run at 20260521_084653_effort-high_lang-en_inj-raw to show the patch worked
analyze_stats_recommendation: Skip this directory by default. analyze_stats.py should detect '.contaminated.json' and exclude the parent directory unless --include-contaminated is passed explicitly.
